docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install 'xpwebapi @ git+https://github.com/devleaks/xplane-webapi.git'

For development, add option dev:

pip install 'xpwebapi[dev] @ git+https://github.com/devleaks/xplane-webapi.git'

docs.title: DatarefMeta
docs.text:
Bases: APIObjMeta

Container for XP Web API dataref meta data

docs.title: is_array property
docs.text: Is dataref an array of values

docs.title: append_index(i)
docs.text:
Add index to list of requested indices for dataref of type array of value

Note from Web API instruction/manual: If you subscribed to certain indexes of the dataref, they’ll be sent in the index order but no sparse arrays will be sent. For example if you subscribed to indexes [1, 5, 7] you’ll get a 3 item array like [200, 200, 200], meaning you need to remember that the first item of that response corresponds to index 1, the second to index 5 and the third to index 7 of the dataref. This also means that if you subscribe to index 2 and later to index 0 you’ll get them as [0,2]. So bottom line is — keep it simple: either ask for a single index, or a range, or all; and if later your requirements change, unsubscribe, then subscribe again.

docs.title: last_indices()
docs.text: Get list of last requested indices

docs.title: save_indices()
docs.text: Keep a copy of indices as requested

docs.title: Cache
docs.text:
Stores dataref or command meta data in cache

Must be "refreshed" each time a new connection is created. Must be refreshed each time a new aircraft is loaded (for new datarefs, commands, etc.) reload_cache() is provided in xpwebapi.

There is no faster structure than a python dict() for (name,value) pair storage.

docs.text: Number of data in cache

docs.title: X-Plane Beacon Monitor
docs.text:
X-Plane «beacon» monitor.

Monitors X-Plane beacon which betrays X-Plane UDP port reachability. Beacon monitor listen for X-Plane beacon on UDP port. When beacon is detected, Beacon Monitor calls back a user-supplied function whenever the reachability status changes.

Usage;

from xpwebapi import XPBeaconMonitor

def callback(connected: bool):
    print("reachable" if connected else "unreachable")

beacon = XPBeaconMonitor()
beacon.set_callback(callback)
beacon.connect()

docs.title: connected property
docs.text: Returns whether beacon from X-Plane is periodically received

docs.title: callback(connected)
docs.text:
Execute callback function if supplied

Callback function prototype

callback(connected: bool)

Connected is True is beacon is detected at regular interval, False otherwise

docs.title: connect()
docs.text: Starts beacon monitor

docs.title: connect_loop()
docs.text: Trys to connect to X-Plane indefinitely until should_not_connect Event is set. If a connection fails, drops, disappears, will try periodically to restore it.

docs.title: disconnect()
docs.text: Terminates beacon monitor

docs.title: find_ip()
docs.text:
Returns first occurence of X-Plane beacon data

Find the IP of XPlane Host in Network. It takes the first one it can find.

BeaconData is a python dataclass with the following attrbiutes:

class BeaconData:
    host: str
    port: int
    hostname: str
    xplane_version: int
    role: int

Returns:

Name Type Description BeaconData BeaconData | None

beacon information or None if not connected/not reachable/no beacon

docs.title: same_host()
docs.text: Attempt to determine if X-Plane is running on local host (where beacon monitor runs) or remote host

docs.title: set_callback(callback=None)
docs.text:
Set callback function

Callback function will be called whenever the status of the "connection" changes.

Callback function prototype

callback(connected: bool)

Connected is True is beacon is detected at regular interval, False otherwise

docs.title: value_type property
docs.text:
Get dataref value type meta data

Valid value types are
  • INTEGER = "int"
  • FLOAT = "float"
  • DOUBLE = "double"
  • INTARRAY = "int_array"
  • FLOATARRAY = "float_array"
  • DATA = "data"

docs.title: write()
docs.text:
Write new value to X-Plane through REST API

Dataref value is saved locally and written to X-Plane when write() or save() is called.

docs.title: Python wrapper for X-Plane Web API
Usage of REST API
docs.text:
import xpwebapi

# REST API
api = xpwebapi.rest_api(host="192.168.1.140", port=8080, api_version="v2")  # defaults: host="127.0.0.1", port=8086, api="/api", use_cache=False

# options: no_cache, version

print(api.capabilities)

api.set_api_version(api_version="v2")

dataref = api.dataref("sim/cockpit2/clock_timer/local_time_seconds")
print(dataref)
# sim/cockpit2/clock_timer/local_time_seconds=42

mapview = api.command("sim/map/show_current")
mapview.execute()

docs.title: Usage of Websocket API
docs.text:
import xpwebapi

ws = xpwebapi.ws_api(host="192.168.1.140", port=8080)  # defaults to v2 for Websocket

def dataref_monitor(dataref: str, value: Any):
    print(f"dataref updated: {dataref}={value}")

def command_active_monitor(command: str, active: bool):
    print(f"command activated: {command}={active}")

ws.on_dataref_update = dataref_monitor
ws.on_command_active = command_active_monitor

ws.connect()
ws.wait_connection() # blocks until X-Plane is reachable

dataref = ws.dataref("sim/cockpit2/clock_timer/local_time_seconds")
ws.monitor_dataref(dataref)
# alternative:
# dataref.monitor()

ws.monitor_command_active(ws.command("sim/map/show_current"))
# alternative:
# command = ws.command("sim/map/show_current")
# command.monitor()

ws.start(release=True)

time.sleep(10)

print("terminating..")
ws.stop()
print("..disconnecting..")
ws.disconnect()
print("..terminated")

docs.title: REST API
docs.text:
Bases: API

XPlane REST API

Adds cache for datarefs and commands meta data.

See Also

X-Plane Web API — REST API

docs.title: capabilities property
docs.text: Fetches API capabilties and caches it

docs.title: connected property
docs.text:
Whether API is reachable

API may not be reachable if
  • X-Plane version before 12.1.4,
  • X-Plane is not running

docs.title: uptime property
docs.text:
Time X-Plane has been running in seconds since start

Value is fetched from simulator dataref sim/time/total_running_time_sec

docs.title: use_cache property writable
docs.text: Use cache for object meta data

docs.title: xp_version property
docs.text: Returns reported X-Plane version from simulator

docs.title: reload_caches(force=False, save=False)
docs.text:
Reload meta data caches

Must be performed regularly, if aircraft changed, etc.

Later, Laminar Research has plan for a notification of additing of datarefs

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default force bool

Force reloading (default: False)

False save bool

Save raw meta data in JSON formatted files (default: False)

False

docs.title: set_api_version(api_version=None)
docs.text:
Set API version

Version is often specified with a v# short string. If no version is supplied, try to take the latest version available. Version numbering is not formally specified, therefore alphabetical ordering of strings if used. Warning: Version v10 < v2.

docs.title: Websocket API
docs.text:

X-Plane Websocket API Client Handler

See https://developer.x-plane.com/article/x-plane-web-api/#Websockets_API.

docs.text: Whether client software is connect to Websoket

Provides request number for Websocket requests

Current request number is available through attribute req_number.

docs.text: URL for the Websocket API

docs.text: Starts attempting to connect to Websocket.

docs.title: connect_loop()
docs.text: Attempts to connect to X-Plane Websocket indefinitely until self.should_not_connect is set. If a connection fails, drops, disappears, will try periodically to restore it.

docs.title: connect_websocket()
docs.text: Create and open Websocket connection if it is reachable

docs.title: disconnect()
docs.text: Stop attempting to connect to Websocket.

docs.title: disconnect_websocket(silent=False)
docs.text: Closes Websocket connection

docs.title: execute(command, duration=0.0)
docs.text:
Execute command in simulator.

Execution is done through REST API if use_rest is True, or Websocket API if use_rest is False and Websocket is opened.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default command Command

Command to execute

required

Returns:

Type Description bool | int

bool if fails

bool | int

request id if succeeded

docs.title: reset_connection()
docs.text:
Reset Websocket connection

Stop existing Websocket connect and create a new one. Initialize and reload cache. If datarefs/commands identifier have changed, reassign new identifiers.
